mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns as investors anticipate future profitability.
failure_condition: The 20-day forward returns of the S&P 500 are negative or flat (within 0.1%) in more than 55% of periods where RSAFS shows positive month-over-month growth.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in the unemployment rate signals a strengthening labor market, leading to increased consumer confidence and spending. This positive economic outlook boosts investor sentiment and drives S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by more than 0.2 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:strong labor market implies higher consumer spending
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of instances where the UNRATE trigger is met during an economic expansion.

mechanism_narrative: High and rising inflation coupled with an increasing federal funds rate indicates an aggressive monetary tightening cycle. This environment typically leads to higher discount rates and concerns about economic slowdown, negatively impacting S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month average growth > 0.5% AND FEDFUNDS 3-month average change > 0.1%
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of periods where both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS show sustained increases as described.

mechanism_narrative: Robust housing starts reflect strong underlying economic activity and consumer confidence, as housing is a significant component of the economy. This positive signal for future economic growth supports positive S&P 500 returns over a short horizon.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of months following a positive month-over-month change in HOUST.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates increasing manufacturing output and capacity utilization, suggesting strong business investment and demand. This positive supply-side signal drives expectations for corporate earnings and S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of periods where the INDPRO growth trigger is met.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent strong job creation, as measured by non-farm payrolls, signifies a healthy and expanding labor market. This fuels consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 returns, particularly when the labor market is already robust.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 35% of triggered instances during periods identified as a strong labor market.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the federal funds rate signals an aggressive monetary tightening stance by the central bank, aimed at curbing inflation. This typically raises borrowing costs, slows economic activity, and negatively impacts equity valuations, leading to lower S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by more than 0.25 percentage points in a single month
evidence_refs: fact:central bank rate hikes impact equity markets
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of periods following a 25bp or greater FEDFUNDS hike.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of declining GDP and rising unemployment indicates a significant economic contraction or recession. This environment severely dampens corporate earnings prospects and investor confidence, leading to substantial negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth < 0% AND UNRATE 3-month average change > 0.3%
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 25% of periods where both GDP is contracting and unemployment is rising as specified.
